key_insight: The paper combines POD-based reduced-order modeling, semi-smooth Newton solvers, and neural-network corrections to efficiently solve nonlinear parametric variational inequalities on a low-dimensional manifold.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster surrogate solvers for computational mechanics problems such as obstacle and frictional contact, while preserving accuracy under strong nonlinear and nonsmooth constraints.
method: Build mixed primal-dual reduced models from POD snapshots of both state and multiplier variables.
Augment truncated POD coordinates with feedforward neural networks to learn nonlinear corrections.
Use hyper-reduction via sparse cubature and greedy nonnegative least squares to cut online residual costs.
evidence_signal: The approach is evaluated on two benchmark variational inequalities—a 2D obstacle problem and a 3D frictional contact problem—comparing full-order, linear reduced, neural-augmented reduced, and hyper-reduced variants.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of snapshot data, reduced basis truncation, and the stability of combining learned corrections with hyper-reduction in nonsmooth Newton iterations.
paper_title: A Hyper-Reduced Neural Network-Augmented Semi-smooth Newton Method for Nonlinear Parametric Variational Inequalities
